text: Hello friends, myself Dr. Renu Singh from Kanpur going to present a video emphasizing the role of laparoscopic conservative dissection with minimal adduceolysis followed by post-op diagnosis for three to two months which facilitates spontaneous conception rates. This patient is a twenty-eight years old female patient with primary infertility and ultrasound revealing bilateral ovarian endometriomas. On laparoscopy we could see a normal size uterus, with bilateral ovarian endometriomas and complete obliteration of powder Douglas. The diluted vasopressin is injected in the uterus and in both the ovarian endometriomas sequentially as and when required in dilution of ten units in hundred ml of normal saline.

The surgeon performs ovarian cystectomy by facilitating an avascular plane of dissection using blunt and sharp dissection with gold scissors, avoiding energy sources to conserve ovarian follicles. Dense adhesions in the pouch of Douglas are cleared, revealing both ovarian endometriomas.

The surgeon performs ovarian cystectomy on the left side using traction and counter traction techniques without energy sources, focusing on preserving the serosa and mesothelial cells. On the right side, the ovarian endometrioma and pouch of Douglas are cleared of dense adhesions using cold scissors and blunt dissection. The ovarian cyst is drained, and cystic knee is performed with traction and counter traction.

The surgeon drains the cyst to expose the cyst wall, avoiding unnecessary dissection such as urethrolysis and retroperitoneal dissection. Saline irrigation is continuously applied to prevent tissue drying and protect peritoneal mesothelial cells. After completing two cystectomies on the right side, a third cyst is revealed and dissected in the appropriate plane.
The surgeon releases significant adhesions of the left sigmoid colon from the lateral pelvic wall. Five hundred milliliters of saline are introduced into the cavity to aid in healing.
